## Summary
DESI data hints that the dark energy equation of state may have crossed the phantom divide (w = -1) from w > -1 in the past to w < -1 today. Single scalar field models cannot achieve this crossing without instabilities, requiring multi-field or non-minimal coupling constructions.

## Evidence
The w0-wa fit to DESI+CMB+SN data places the crossing at z ~ 0.4, though the significance depends on supernova sample choice. Theoretical models achieving phantom crossing include quintom (two-field), k-essence with non-canonical kinetic terms, and f(R) gravity effective dark energy.

## Significance
If real, phantom crossing would rule out all single minimally-coupled scalar field dark energy models, dramatically narrowing the theoretical landscape. It could also signal an effective description of modified gravity rather than a true dark energy component.
